summary: Verified the three NEW UI features from this wave: ScribePanel 'Send to claim' (code-review + backend pytest), NLBookCard reschedule + cancel intents (live UI E2E), and AITemplatesPage paginated provider dropdown (live UI E2E). Backend test_third_wave_ai.py runs 8 passed / 4 skipped (skips are happy-paths needing seeded encounter/audio — same as last iteration). All three frontend flows are wired correctly with proper data-testids, intent-aware UI, and graceful clarification messages. No regressions detected on already-green features.
critical_code_review_comments: ScribePanel.jsx Send-to-claim: the 'Create draft claim' submit is gated only on payerId + non-empty cpt/icd lists (good). However the doctor cannot edit the suggested CPT units / billed_cents / modifiers before posting — every line goes in at units=1 and billed_cents=0. That matches the backend contract but means the doctor must reopen the draft claim afterwards to set fees. Minor UX gap, not a bug.

NLBookCard.jsx: when intent is 'reschedule' the patient/provider/duration inputs remain visible above the Start field but are not used by the /nl/reschedule call (only target_appointment_id + start_iso + duration_minutes are sent). This is correct behavior, but the irrelevant Patient/Provider selects shown on a reschedule confirm screen could confuse staff. Consider hiding non-applicable fields when intent !== 'create'.

AITemplatesPage.jsx: the 'pagination' is actually client-side filter + slice(0,100) on the full provider list. Works fine at the current 54-doctor scale, but if a tenant ever exceeds ~500 providers the initial GET /api/auth/users?role=doctor will get heavy. A future server-side ?q= query param would be a cleaner long-term solution.
scribe sendToClaim() silently sets billed_cents:0 — recommend pulling fee schedule for the selected payer or warning the user.

success_rate.backend: 100% (8/8 active tests pass; 4 happy-path skips need seeded scribe encounter)

seed_data_creation: None (used existing Riverbend demo seed: Hannah Whitaker has recurring 10:00 AM appointments with Dr. Noah Carter, used as reschedule/cancel target).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All three wave features verified live: (1) AITemplatesPage at /settings/ai-templates -- Provider scope shows search box (because 54 docs > 50 threshold), filter works ('noah' -> 1 result, 'license' -> 20 results), no overflow hint since count <= 100; (2) NLBookCard at /scheduling -- typing 'reschedule Hannah Whitaker's Friday adjustment to next Monday at 11am' returns intent=reschedule, confidence=HIGH, target_id=22e3e71c..., patient pre-filled 'Hannah Whitaker', start pre-filled 05/11/2026 11:00 AM, confirm-btn label='Reschedule' (calendar-clock icon); typing 'cancel Hannah's Friday appointment' returns intent=cancel, confirm-btn label='Cancel appointment' (calendar-x icon), shows clarifications when patient name is ambiguous; (3) ScribePanel send-to-claim: backend pytest 5/5 active assertions pass (role gate, 404 unknown note, 422 no codes, 404 unknown payer, happy-path creates claim). UI wiring reviewed -- shows payer Select + Create draft claim button only when both cpt_suggestions and icd_suggestions are non-empty; renders <a href=/billing/claims/{id}> success link. No mocked APIs.
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found this iteration. The single 'flaky' read in my chained test (RESCHEDULE intent first reported as 'create') was a test-script timing issue: I read the badge before the Claude /parse call had returned. Re-running RESCHEDULE in isolation with a 12s wait yielded intent=reschedule on first read.
